  have h := dep_policy OliveGarden managed_og
  cases h with
  | inl hEq =>
      have h2 : SecurityDeposit OliveGarden = 2000 := by
        simpa [rent_og] using hEq
      exact Or.inl h2
  | inr hLt =>
      have h2 : 2000 < SecurityDeposit OliveGarden := by
        simpa [rent_og] using hLt
      exact Or.inr h2
</lean>

axiom Show : Type
axiom StrangerThings : Show
axiom BlackMirror : Show

axiom Popular : Show → Prop
axiom Netflix : Show → Prop
axiom Binge : Show → Prop            -- Karen binge-watches the show
axiom Download : Show → Prop         -- Karen downloads the show
axiom ShareWithLisa : Show → Prop    -- Karen shares the show with Lisa

-- "Stranger Things" is a popular Netflix show.
axiom ST_netflix : Netflix StrangerThings
axiom ST_popular : Popular StrangerThings

-- If a Netflix show is popular, Karen will binge-watch it.
axiom R_np_binge : ∀ s : Show, Netflix s → Popular s → Binge s

-- If and only if Karen binge-watches a Netflix show, she will download it.
axiom R_iff_binge_download : ∀ s : Show, Netflix s → (Binge s ↔ Download s)

-- Karen does not download "Black Mirror."
axiom BM_not_download : ¬ Download BlackMirror

-- "Black Mirror" is a Netflix show.
axiom BM_netflix : Netflix BlackMirror

-- If Karen binge-watches a Netflix show, she will share it with Lisa.
axiom R_binge_share : ∀ s : Show, Netflix s → Binge s → ShareWithLisa s

-- From the iff and not-download, we can deduce Karen does not binge-watch Black Mirror.
theorem BM_not_binge : ¬ Binge BlackMirror :=
by
  intro hB
  have hN : Netflix BlackMirror := BM_netflix
  have hiff : Binge BlackMirror ↔ Download BlackMirror := R_iff_binge_download BlackMirror hN
  have hD : Download BlackMirror := (Iff.mp hiff) hB
  exact BM_not_download hD

-- We cannot derive ShareWithLisa BlackMirror from the given axioms,
-- nor can we derive its negation.
-- (We have Netflix BlackMirror, but we have ¬Binge BlackMirror,
-- and the only rule to get sharing requires Binge.)
</lean>

axiom WellPaid : obj → Prop
axiom LivesInTaxHaven : obj → Prop

-- Everyone at the mixer is a Grand Slam champion or an Oscar-nominated actor.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, At x → (GrandSlamChampion x ∨ OscarNominatedActor x)
-- Every Grand Slam champion at the mixer is a professional tennis player.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, At x → GrandSlamChampion x → ProfessionalTennisPlayer x
-- All Oscar-nominated actors at the mixer are celebrities.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, At x → OscarNominatedActor x → Celebrity x
-- All professional tennis players at the mixer are athletes.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, At x → ProfessionalTennisPlayer x → Athlete x
-- If a person at the mixer is a celebrity, then they are well paid.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, At x → Celebrity x → WellPaid x
-- If a person at the mixer is an athlete, then they are famous.
axiom A6 : ∀ x : obj, At x → Athlete x → Famous x
-- All well-paid people at the mixer live in tax havens.
axiom A7 : ∀ x : obj, At x → WellPaid x → LivesInTaxHaven x

-- Djokovic is at the mixer
axiom A8 : At Djokovic
-- If Djokovic is a famous athlete, then Djokovic is well-paid.
axiom A9 : (Famous Djokovic ∧ Athlete Djokovic) → WellPaid Djokovic

-- We can derive that Djokovic is well-paid (but not necessarily that he is a Grand Slam champion).
theorem djokovic_well_paid : WellPaid Djokovic :=
by
  have disj : GrandSlamChampion Djokovic ∨ OscarNominatedActor Djokovic := (A1 Djokovic) A8
  cases disj with
  | inl hG =>
      have hPTP : ProfessionalTennisPlayer Djokovic := (A2 Djokovic) A8 hG
      have hAth : Athlete Djokovic := (A4 Djokovic) A8 hPTP
      have hFam : Famous Djokovic := (A6 Djokovic) A8 hAth
      exact A9 ⟨hFam, hAth⟩
  | inr hO =>
      have hCel : Celebrity Djokovic := (A3 Djokovic) A8 hO
      exact (A5 Djokovic) A8 hCel

theorem djokovic_tax_haven : LivesInTaxHaven Djokovic :=
by
  apply (A7 Djokovic) A8
  exact djokovic_well_paid

-- From the given axioms, we cannot prove GrandSlamChampion Djokovic nor its negation.
</lean>

axiom Person : Type
axiom Taylor : Person

axiom ESR : Person → Prop -- eats salads regularly
axiom VCH : Person → Prop -- very conscious about health and eating habits
axiom GHC : Person → Prop -- grew up in health-conscious childhood home
axiom FDN : Person → Prop -- fulfill daily nutritional intakes
axiom DPW : Person → Prop -- disregard physical well-being
axiom VG  : Person → Prop -- visit the gym at least once a day

-- Rules
axiom R1 : ∀ x : Person, ESR x → VCH x
axiom R2 : ∀ x : Person, GHC x → ESR x
axiom R3 : ∀ x : Person, FDN x → GHC x
axiom R4 : ∀ x : Person, DPW x → ¬VCH x
axiom R5 : ∀ x : Person, VG x → FDN x

-- Taylor's given disjunction
axiom Taylor_case : (GHC Taylor ∧ DPW Taylor) ∨ (¬GHC Taylor ∧ ¬DPW Taylor)

theorem taylor_neither_ghc_nor_vg : ¬GHC Taylor ∧ ¬VG Taylor := by
  -- Show Taylor cannot both have GHC and DPW
  have not_both : ¬(GHC Taylor ∧ DPW Taylor) := by
    intro h
    have hG : GHC Taylor := h.left
    have hD : DPW Taylor := h.right
    have hE : ESR Taylor := R2 Taylor hG
    have hV : VCH Taylor := R1 Taylor hE
    have hNV : ¬VCH Taylor := R4 Taylor hD
    exact hNV hV
  -- From the given disjunction and impossibility of the first case, deduce the second case
  have hNotHCNotDPW : ¬GHC Taylor ∧ ¬DPW Taylor := by
    cases Taylor_case with
    | inl hgd =>
        exact False.elim (not_both hgd)
    | inr hnn =>
        exact hnn
  have notHC : ¬GHC Taylor := hNotHCNotDPW.left
  -- From ¬GHC and (VG → FDN → GHC), get ¬VG
  have notVG : ¬VG Taylor := by
    intro hVG
    have hGHC : GHC Taylor := R3 Taylor (R5 Taylor hVG)
    exact notHC hGHC
  exact And.intro notHC notVG
</lean>

<lean>
axiom Person : Type
axiom Taylor : Person

axiom EatsSaladsRegularly : Person → Prop
axiom VeryConscious : Person → Prop
axiom GrewUpHealthConsciousHome : Person → Prop
axiom FulfillDailyNutritionalIntakes : Person → Prop
axiom DisregardPhysicalWellBeing : Person → Prop
axiom VisitsGymAtLeastOnceADay : Person → Prop

-- All people who eat salads regularly are very conscious.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : Person, EatsSaladsRegularly x → VeryConscious x
-- All people who grew up in health-conscious childhood homes eat salads regularly.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : Person, GrewUpHealthConsciousHome x → EatsSaladsRegularly x
-- All people who fulfill their daily nutritional intakes grew up in health-conscious childhood homes.
axiom R3 : ∀ x : Person, FulfillDailyNutritionalIntakes x → GrewUpHealthConsciousHome x
-- All people who disregard their physical well-being are not very conscious.
axiom R4 : ∀ x : Person, DisregardPhysicalWellBeing x → ¬ VeryConscious x
-- If people visit the gym at least once a day, then they fulfill their daily nutritional intakes.
axiom R5 : ∀ x : Person, VisitsGymAtLeastOnceADay x → FulfillDailyNutritionalIntakes x
-- Taylor either (grew up in a health-conscious home and disregards well-being) or (neither).
axiom R6 :
  (GrewUpHealthConsciousHome Taylor ∧ DisregardPhysicalWellBeing Taylor) ∨
  (¬GrewUpHealthConsciousHome Taylor ∧ ¬DisregardPhysicalWellBeing Taylor)

-- From growing up in a health-conscious home, one is very conscious.
lemma grewUp_implies_very : ∀ x : Person, GrewUpHealthConsciousHome x → VeryConscious x :=
  fun x hx => R1 x (R2 x hx)

-- It is impossible that Taylor both grew up health-conscious and disregards well-being.
lemma not_both_grewup_and_disregard :
  ¬ (GrewUpHealthConsciousHome Taylor ∧ DisregardPhysicalWellBeing Taylor) :=
by
  intro h
  have hg : GrewUpHealthConsciousHome Taylor := h.left
  have hd : DisregardPhysicalWellBeing Taylor := h.right
  have hv : VeryConscious Taylor := grewUp_implies_very Taylor hg
  have hnv : ¬ VeryConscious Taylor := R4 Taylor hd
  exact hnv hv

-- Therefore the second disjunct must hold: Taylor did neither.
lemma not_grewup_and_not_disregard :
  (¬ GrewUpHealthConsciousHome Taylor ∧ ¬ DisregardPhysicalWellBeing Taylor) :=
by
  have hdisj := R6
  cases hdisj with
  | inl h1 => exact False.elim (not_both_grewup_and_disregard h1)
  | inr h2 => exact h2

-- Conclude Taylor does not visit the gym at least once a day.
theorem taylor_not_visit : ¬ VisitsGymAtLeastOnceADay Taylor :=
by
  intro hvisit
  have hfulfill : FulfillDailyNutritionalIntakes Taylor := R5 Taylor hvisit
  have hgrow : GrewUpHealthConsciousHome Taylor := R3 Taylor hfulfill
  have hnotgrow : ¬ GrewUpHealthConsciousHome Taylor := (not_grewup_and_not_disregard).left
  exact hnotgrow hgrow
</lean>

-- All people who eat salads regularly are very conscious.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : Person, EatsSaladsRegularly x → VeryConscious x
-- All people who grew up in health-conscious childhood homes eat salads regularly.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : Person, GrewUpHealthConsciousHome x → EatsSaladsRegularly x
-- All people who fulfill their daily nutritional intakes grew up in health-conscious childhood homes.
axiom R3 : ∀ x : Person, FulfillDailyNutritionalIntakes x → GrewUpHealthConsciousHome x
-- All people who disregard their physical well-being are not very conscious.
axiom R4 : ∀ x : Person, DisregardPhysicalWellBeing x → ¬ VeryConscious x
-- If people visit the gym at least once a day, then they fulfill their daily nutritional intakes.
axiom R5 : ∀ x : Person, VisitsGymAtLeastOnceADay x → FulfillDailyNutritionalIntakes x
-- Taylor either (grew up in a health-conscious home and disregards well-being) or (neither).
axiom R6 :
  (GrewUpHealthConsciousHome Taylor ∧ DisregardPhysicalWellBeing Taylor) ∨
  (¬GrewUpHealthConsciousHome Taylor ∧ ¬DisregardPhysicalWellBeing Taylor)

-- From growing up in a health-conscious home, one is very conscious.
lemma grewUp_implies_very : ∀ x : Person, GrewUpHealthConsciousHome x → VeryConscious x :=
  fun x hx => R1 x (R2 x hx)

-- It is impossible that Taylor both grew up health-conscious and disregards well-being.
lemma not_both_grewup_and_disregard :
  ¬ (GrewUpHealthConsciousHome Taylor ∧ DisregardPhysicalWellBeing Taylor) :=
by
  intro h
  have hg : GrewUpHealthConsciousHome Taylor := h.left
  have hd : DisregardPhysicalWellBeing Taylor := h.right
  have hv : VeryConscious Taylor := grewUp_implies_very Taylor hg
  have hnv : ¬ VeryConscious Taylor := R4 Taylor hd
  exact hnv hv

-- Therefore the second disjunct must hold: Taylor did neither.
lemma not_grewup_and_not_disregard :
  (¬ GrewUpHealthConsciousHome Taylor ∧ ¬ DisregardPhysicalWellBeing Taylor) :=
by
  have hdisj := R6
  cases hdisj with
  | inl h1 => exact False.elim (not_both_grewup_and_disregard h1)
  | inr h2 => exact h2

-- Conclude Taylor does not visit the gym at least once a day.
theorem taylor_not_visit : ¬ VisitsGymAtLeastOnceADay Taylor :=
by
  intro hvisit
  have hfulfill : FulfillDailyNutritionalIntakes Taylor := R5 Taylor hvisit
  have hgrow : GrewUpHealthConsciousHome Taylor := R3 Taylor hfulfill
  have hnotgrow : ¬ GrewUpHealthConsciousHome Taylor := (not_grewup_and_not_disregard).left
  exact hnotgrow hgrow

axiom HangPlay : Person → Prop
axiom SiblingsBornTogether : Person → Prop

axiom LiveAtHome : Person → Prop
axiom LiveWithStrangers : Person → Prop
axiom Baby : Person → Prop

-- Rules from the context
axiom R1 : ∀ x : Person, MultipleBirth x → HangPlay x
axiom R2 : ∀ x : Person, SiblingsBornTogether x → MultipleBirth x
axiom R3 : ∀ x : Person, Complain x → SiblingsBornTogether x
axiom R4 : ∀ x : Person, LiveAtHome x → ¬ LiveWithStrangers x
axiom R5 : ∀ x : Person, HangPlay x → LiveAtHome x

-- Luke's disjunction:
-- Either (Baby ∧ MultipleBirth ∧ LiveWithStrangers) or (¬(Baby ∧ MultipleBirth) ∧ ¬LiveWithStrangers)
axiom Luke_choice :
  (Baby Luke ∧ MultipleBirth Luke ∧ LiveWithStrangers Luke) ∨
  (¬ (Baby Luke ∧ MultipleBirth Luke) ∧ ¬ LiveWithStrangers Luke)

-- Consequence: being born in a multiple birth implies not living with strangers
theorem MB_implies_not_LWS : ∀ x : Person, MultipleBirth x → ¬ LiveWithStrangers x := by
  intro x hmb
  have hhp := R1 x hmb
  have hhome := R5 x hhp
  exact R4 x hhome

-- Hence the first disjunct for Luke is impossible
theorem eliminate_case_A : ¬ (Baby Luke ∧ MultipleBirth Luke ∧ LiveWithStrangers Luke) := by
  intro h
  cases h with
  | intro hb rest =>
    cases rest with
    | intro hmb hlws =>
      have hn := MB_implies_not_LWS Luke hmb
      exact hn hlws

-- Therefore, the second disjunct must hold
theorem choose_case_B : (¬ (Baby Luke ∧ MultipleBirth Luke) ∧ ¬ LiveWithStrangers Luke) := by
  have hAorB := Luke_choice
  have hnotA := eliminate_case_A
  cases hAorB with
  | inl hA => exact False.elim (hnotA hA)
  | inr hB => exact hB

-- nor can we derive ¬ Complain Luke (no axioms forbid complaining; they only give consequences).

<lean>
axiom obj : Type
axiom ETS : obj
axiom Tom : obj

axiom TakingGRE : obj → Prop
axiom ApplicantGRE : obj → Prop
axiom ProvidesAidBy : obj → obj → Prop
axiom ProvesHardship : obj → Prop
axiom SingleParentFamily : obj → Prop
axiom FewResources : obj → Prop

-- Rules from the context
axiom aid_rule : ∀ x : obj, ApplicantGRE x → ProvesHardship x → ProvidesAidBy ETS x
axiom hardship_single : ∀ x : obj, SingleParentFamily x → ProvesHardship x
axiom hardship_few : ∀ x : obj, FewResources x → ProvesHardship x
axiom applicant_taking : ∀ x : obj, ApplicantGRE x → TakingGRE x

-- Facts about Tom
axiom tom_single : SingleParentFamily Tom
axiom tom_few : FewResources Tom
axiom tom_applicant : ApplicantGRE Tom

-- Derivations
theorem tom_hardship : ProvesHardship Tom :=
  hardship_single Tom tom_single

theorem tom_aid : ProvidesAidBy ETS Tom :=
  aid_rule Tom tom_applicant tom_hardship

theorem tom_taking : TakingGRE Tom :=
  applicant_taking Tom tom_applicant

-- The claim in question: "No one taking the GRE test has financial aid provided to them by something."
-- We prove its negation by exhibiting Tom as a counterexample.
theorem claim_false :
  ¬ (∀ x : obj, TakingGRE x → ¬ ∃ y : obj, ProvidesAidBy y x) := by
  intro h
  have hTom : ¬ ∃ y : obj, ProvidesAidBy y Tom := h Tom tom_taking
  have ex : ∃ y : obj, ProvidesAidBy y Tom := ⟨ETS, tom_aid⟩
  exact hTom ex
</lean>

ANSWER: False

axiom NiceToAnimals : obj → Prop
axiom MeanToAnimals : obj → Prop
axiom Grumpy : obj → Prop
axiom LovesAnimals : obj → Prop
axiom PetOwner : obj → Prop

-- No one nice to animals is also mean to animals.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, NiceToAnimals x → ¬ MeanToAnimals x
-- Some grumpy people are mean to animals.
axiom A2 : ∃ x : obj, Grumpy x ∧ MeanToAnimals x
-- All animal lovers are nice to animals.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, LovesAnimals x → NiceToAnimals x
-- All pet owners love animals.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, PetOwner x → LovesAnimals x
-- Tom is a pet owner.
axiom A5 : PetOwner Tom

theorem tom_loves : LovesAnimals Tom := by
  exact A4 Tom A5

theorem tom_nice : NiceToAnimals Tom := by
  exact A3 Tom tom_loves

theorem tom_not_mean : ¬ MeanToAnimals Tom := by
  exact A1 Tom tom_nice

-- From the given axioms, we cannot derive Grumpy Tom or ¬ Grumpy Tom.
-- The existence of some grumpy mean person does not imply Tom is grumpy,
-- and ¬ MeanToAnimals Tom does not imply ¬ Grumpy Tom.
-- Therefore, the truth of "Grumpy Tom" is underdetermined.
</lean>

  intro h

<lean>
axiom obj : Type
axiom Peter : obj

axiom Spill : obj → Prop
axiom Tidy : obj → Prop
axiom Cleanly : obj → Prop
axiom ValueOrder : obj → Prop
axiom FamilyPrioritize : obj → Prop
axiom CFG : obj → Prop  -- clumsy foodie who goes out frequently to find new food restaurants to try

-- If people spill a lot of food on their clothing, then they are not notably tidy.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Spill x → ¬ Tidy x
-- All clumsy foodies who go out frequently to find new food restaurants to try spill a lot of food.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, CFG x → Spill x
-- If people are cleanly, then they are notably tidy.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Cleanly x → Tidy x
-- If people value order and spotlessness, then they are cleanly.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, ValueOrder x → Cleanly x
-- All people whose families prioritize order and spotlessness themselves value order and spotlessness.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, FamilyPrioritize x → ValueOrder x
-- Peter is either (spill ∧ cleanly) or (¬spill ∧ ¬cleanly).
axiom A6 : (Spill Peter ∧ Cleanly Peter) ∨ (¬ Spill Peter ∧ ¬ Cleanly Peter)

theorem peter_either_both_or_neither :
  (CFG Peter ∧ FamilyPrioritize Peter) ∨ (¬ CFG Peter ∧ ¬ FamilyPrioritize Peter) := by
  -- Show Peter cannot both spill and be cleanly
  have hNotBoth : ¬ (Spill Peter ∧ Cleanly Peter) := by
    intro h
    have hs : Spill Peter := h.left
    have hc : Cleanly Peter := h.right
    have ht : Tidy Peter := A3 Peter hc
    have hnt : ¬ Tidy Peter := A1 Peter hs
    exact hnt ht
  -- From A6 and impossibility of the left disjunct, deduce ¬Spill Peter ∧ ¬Cleanly Peter
  have hnsnc : ¬ Spill Peter ∧ ¬ Cleanly Peter := by
    apply Or.elim A6
    · intro h
      exact False.elim (hNotBoth h)
    · intro h
      exact h
  have hns : ¬ Spill Peter := hnsnc.left
  have hnc : ¬ Cleanly Peter := hnsnc.right
  -- From CFG → Spill and ¬Spill, deduce ¬CFG
  have notCFG : ¬ CFG Peter := by
    intro hcfg
    have hs : Spill Peter := A2 Peter hcfg
    exact hns hs
  -- From FamilyPrioritize → ValueOrder → Cleanly and ¬Cleanly, deduce ¬FamilyPrioritize
  have fp_to_clean : FamilyPrioritize Peter → Cleanly Peter := by
    intro hfp
    have hv : ValueOrder Peter := A5 Peter hfp
    exact A4 Peter hv
  have notFP : ¬ FamilyPrioritize Peter := by
    intro hfp
    exact hnc (fp_to_clean hfp)
  -- Conclude the right disjunct
  exact Or.inr (And.intro notCFG notFP)
</lean>

ANSWER: True

axiom Dreamy : obj → Prop

-- All of Zaha Hadid's design styles that Max adores have interesting geometries.

-- If a design by Max that he adores has interesting geometries, then the design is a brutalist building and evocative.
axiom a6 : ∀ x : obj, DesignByMax x ∧ Adores x ∧ Interesting x → Brutalist x ∧ Evocative x

-- A useful consequence: there is no design by Max that he adores which also has interesting geometries.
theorem no_adored_interesting_design_by_max :
  ∀ x : obj, DesignByMax x → Adores x → Interesting x → False :=

  intro x
  intro hx
  intro hA
  intro hI
  have h := a6 x ⟨hx, hA, hI⟩
  have hB : Brutalist x := And.left h
  have hNotI : ¬ Interesting x := a2 x ⟨hB, hA⟩
  exact hNotI hI

-- From the given axioms, we cannot derive this existential claim,
-- nor can we derive its negation (there might be a design by Max that is brutalist but not adored).
</lean>

axiom obj : Type

axiom DesignByMax : obj → Prop
axiom Adores : obj → Prop
axiom ZahaStyle : obj → Prop
axiom KellyStyle : obj → Prop
axiom Interesting : obj → Prop
axiom Brutalist : obj → Prop
axiom Evocative : obj → Prop
axiom Dreamy : obj → Prop

-- All Zaha Hadid styles that Max adores are interesting
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, ZahaStyle x ∧ Adores x → Interesting x
-- No brutalist buildings that Max adores are interesting
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Brutalist x ∧ Adores x → ¬ Interesting x
-- Every style that Max adores is either Zaha's or Kelly's
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Adores x → (ZahaStyle x ∨ KellyStyle x)
-- All Kelly styles that Max adores are evocative
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, KellyStyle x ∧ Adores x → Evocative x
-- All Kelly styles that Max adores are dreamy
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, KellyStyle x ∧ Adores x → Dreamy x
-- If a design by Max that he adores is interesting, then it is brutalist and evocative
axiom A6 : ∀ x : obj, DesignByMax x ∧ Adores x ∧ Interesting x → Brutalist x ∧ Evocative x

-- Target (existential) would be: ∃ x, DesignByMax x ∧ Evocative x ∧ Dreamy x
-- From the given axioms, there is no existence premise linking DesignByMax to anything Max adores,
-- nor any existence of adored items. So we cannot prove existence or its negation.

-- However, we can derive a consistency lemma showing there are no designs by Max
-- that he adores which are interesting (since that would lead to a contradiction with A2).
theorem no_design_by_max_adored_interesting : ∀ x : obj, ¬ (DesignByMax x ∧ Adores x ∧ Interesting x) := by
  intro x
  intro h
  have hAdoresInteresting : Adores x ∧ Interesting x := And.right h
  have hAdores : Adores x := And.left hAdoresInteresting
  have hInteresting : Interesting x := And.right hAdoresInteresting
  have hBrutalistAndEvocative : Brutalist x ∧ Evocative x := A6 x h
  have hBrutalist : Brutalist x := And.left hBrutalistAndEvocative
  have hNotInteresting : ¬ Interesting x := A2 x (And.intro hBrutalist hAdores)
  exact hNotInteresting hInteresting
</lean>

axiom HardingMine : obj

axiom Mine : obj → Prop

axiom Donated : obj → Prop

axiom PM_is_mountain : MountainRange Picuris
axiom PM_location : InNewMexico Picuris ∨ InTexas Picuris
axiom Onate_visited_PM : Visited Onate Picuris
axiom HM_is_mine : Mine HardingMine
axiom HM_in_PM : LocatedIn HardingMine Picuris
axiom HM_donated : Donated HardingMine

-- No Texas mountain range has a mine that has been donated
axiom no_TX_range_with_donated_mine :
  ∀ r : obj, MountainRange r → InTexas r → ¬ (∃ m : obj, Mine m ∧ LocatedIn m r ∧ Donated m)

-- From the donated mine in Picuris, deduce Picuris is not in Texas
theorem not_in_texas_picuris : ¬ InTexas Picuris := by
  intro htx
  have hnot : ¬ (∃ m, Mine m ∧ LocatedIn m Picuris ∧ Donated m) :=
    no_TX_range_with_donated_mine Picuris PM_is_mountain htx
  have hex : ∃ m, Mine m ∧ LocatedIn m Picuris ∧ Donated m := by
    refine Exists.intro HardingMine ?_
    exact And.intro HM_is_mine (And.intro HM_in_PM HM_donated)
  exact hnot hex

-- From "in New Mexico or Texas" and not in Texas, we can conclude in New Mexico
theorem in_new_mexico_picuris : InNewMexico Picuris := by
  cases PM_location with
  | inl hnm => exact hnm
  | inr htx =>
    exact False.elim (not_in_texas_picuris htx)

-- Target claim: "Juan de Onate visited a mountain range in Texas."
def Claim : Prop := ∃ r : obj, MountainRange r ∧ InTexas r ∧ Visited Onate r

-- From the given information, we cannot prove Claim or its negation.
-- We only know Onate visited Picuris, and Picuris is not in Texas.
-- There is no information excluding the possibility that Onate visited some other Texas mountain range.
-- Therefore, neither Claim nor ¬Claim is derivable from the axioms.
</lean>

axiom MostActive : obj → Prop

axiom LostToRafa : obj → Prop
axiom AtRG : obj → Prop     -- at Roland Garros 2022

-- If someone is ranked highly by the WTA, then they are among the most active (Grand-Slam/major) players.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, RankedWTA x → MostActive x
-- Everyone who lost to Iga at Roland Garros 2022 is ranked highly by the WTA.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, LostToIga x → RankedWTA x
-- All female tennis players at Roland Garros 2022 lost to Iga Świątek.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Female x → LostToIga x
-- All tennis players at Roland Garros 2022 are either female or male.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, AtRG x → Female x ∨ Male x
-- All male tennis players at Roland Garros 2022 lost to Rafael Nadal.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Male x → LostToRafa x
-- If Coco is ranked highly by the WTA or lost to Rafael Nadal, then Coco is not a male tennis player at Roland Garros 2022.
axiom A6 : (RankedWTA Coco ∨ LostToRafa Coco) → ¬ Male Coco
-- Coco Gauff is at Roland Garros 2022.
axiom A7 : AtRG Coco

theorem coco_most_active : MostActive Coco := by
  -- First, show Coco is not male.
  have hNotMale : ¬ Male Coco := by
    intro hMale
    have hLoseRafa : LostToRafa Coco := A5 Coco hMale
    have hNoMale : ¬ Male Coco := A6 (Or.inr hLoseRafa)
    exact hNoMale hMale
  -- From being at RG and not male, deduce Coco is female.
  have hFemOrMale : Female Coco ∨ Male Coco := A4 Coco A7
  have hFemale : Female Coco := by
    cases hFemOrMale with
    | inl hf => exact hf
    | inr hm => exact False.elim (hNotMale hm)
  -- Female ⇒ lost to Iga ⇒ ranked highly ⇒ most active.
  have hLoseIga : LostToIga Coco := A3 Coco hFemale
  have hRanked : RankedWTA Coco := A2 Coco hLoseIga
  exact A1 Coco hRanked
</lean>

  have hl : LeapsStraight x := jump_implies_leap x hj
  exact leap_implies_activate x hl

  exact yuri_not_activate this

-- Some professional basketball players are not American nationals.
axiom ex_not_american_pbp : ∃ x : obj, ProfessionalBasketballPlayer x ∧ ¬ AmericanNational x

axiom pbp_implies_cjs : ∀ x : obj, ProfessionalBasketballPlayer x → CanJumpShots x

-- If someone can jump shots, they leap straight into the air.
axiom cjs_implies_ls : ∀ x : obj, CanJumpShots x → LeapsStraight x

-- If someone leaps straight into the air, they activate their leg muscles.
axiom ls_implies_alm : ∀ x : obj, LeapsStraight x → ActivatesLegMuscles x

-- Yuri does not activate his leg muscles.
axiom yuri_not_alm : ¬ ActivatesLegMuscles Yuri

-- We prove the negation of the statement:
-- If Yuri is not an American professional basketball player, then Yuri is a professional basketball player.
theorem not_statement : ¬ ( (¬ (AmericanNational Yuri ∧ ProfessionalBasketballPlayer Yuri)) → ProfessionalBasketballPlayer Yuri ) :=
  fun h =>
    -- First, show Yuri is not a professional basketball player
    let pbp_to_alm : ProfessionalBasketballPlayer Yuri → ActivatesLegMuscles Yuri :=
      fun hp =>
        ls_implies_alm Yuri (cjs_implies_ls Yuri (pbp_implies_cjs Yuri hp))
    have not_pbp : ¬ ProfessionalBasketballPlayer Yuri :=
      fun hp => yuri_not_alm (pbp_to_alm hp)
    -- Antecedent holds because Yuri is not a professional basketball player
    have antecedent : ¬ (AmericanNational Yuri ∧ ProfessionalBasketballPlayer Yuri) :=
      fun h' => not_pbp h'.right
    -- Using the implication, we get Yuri is a professional basketball player, contradiction
    have hp : ProfessionalBasketballPlayer Yuri := h antecedent
    show False from not_pbp hp

axiom pro_jump : ∀ x : obj, Pro x → CanJump x

axiom yuri_not_activate : Activate Yuri → False

-- Queried implication: (¬ Leap Yuri) → (American Yuri ∧ Pro Yuri)
-- We avoid '¬' and write it as (Leap Yuri → False).
theorem yuri_statement_false :
  ((Leap Yuri → False) → (American Yuri ∧ Pro Yuri)) → False := by
  intro hImp
  -- From the chain Pro → CanJump → Leap → Activate and ¬Activate Yuri we get ¬Pro Yuri

axiom Likes : obj → obj → Prop

axiom KiKi : obj

-- All dogs are animals that bark.

-- If an animal has legs, then it jumps.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, (Animal x ∧ HasLegs x) → Jumps x
-- If an animal is terricolous, then it has legs.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, (Animal x ∧ Terricolous x) → HasLegs x
-- KiKi is an animal.
axiom A6 : Animal KiKi
-- If KiKi neither jumps nor has legs, then KiKi is terricolous or has legs.
axiom A7 : (¬ Jumps KiKi ∧ ¬ HasLegs KiKi) → (Terricolous KiKi ∨ HasLegs KiKi)

-- Some derived lemmas (do not establish Terricolous KiKi)
lemma legs_of_terr {x : obj} (Ax : Animal x) (Tx : Terricolous x) : HasLegs x :=
  (A5 x) (And.intro Ax Tx)

lemma jumps_of_legs {x : obj} (Ax : Animal x) (Lx : HasLegs x) : Jumps x :=
  (A4 x) (And.intro Ax Lx)

lemma jumps_of_terricolous_kiki (T : Terricolous KiKi) : Jumps KiKi :=
  jumps_of_legs A6 (legs_of_terr A6 T)

-- Cannot prove Terricolous KiKi or ¬ Terricolous KiKi from given axioms.
</lean>

theorem well_attended_implies_engaged : ∀ x : obj, WellAttended x → Engaged x := by
  intro x hx
  have hio : InvitesOthers x := A3 x hx
  have hmw : MakesWeddingPlans x := A2 x hio
  exact A1 x hmw

iterations.llm_response:
<lean>
axiom obj : Type
axiom KiKi : obj

axiom Animal : obj → Prop
axiom Dog : obj → Prop
axiom Bark : obj → Prop
axiom Jump : obj → Prop
axiom Legs : obj → Prop
axiom Terricolous : obj → Prop
axiom LikesJane : obj → Prop

-- Jane does not like any animals that bark.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, (Animal x ∧ Bark x) → ¬ LikesJane x
-- All dogs are animals that bark.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Dog x → (Animal x ∧ Bark x)
-- Jane likes all animals that jump.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, (Animal x ∧ Jump x) → LikesJane x
-- If an animal has legs, then it jumps.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, (Animal x ∧ Legs x) → Jump x
-- If an animal is terricolous, then it has legs.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, (Animal x ∧ Terricolous x) → Legs x
-- KiKi is an animal.
axiom A6 : Animal KiKi
-- If KiKi neither jumps nor has legs, then KiKi is terricolous or has legs.
axiom A7 : (¬ Jump KiKi ∧ ¬ Legs KiKi) → (Terricolous KiKi ∨ Legs KiKi)

open Classical

theorem kiki_not_bark_and_not_dog : ¬ Bark KiKi ∧ ¬ Dog KiKi := by
  -- First, show KiKi jumps.
  have kiki_jumps : Jump KiKi := by
    by_contra hNJ
    by_cases hL : Legs KiKi
    · -- If KiKi has legs, then as an animal KiKi jumps: contradiction.
      have : Jump KiKi := A4 KiKi (And.intro A6 hL)
      exact hNJ this
    · -- If KiKi does not have legs, use the given implication to derive a contradiction.
      have hdisj : Terricolous KiKi ∨ Legs KiKi := A7 (And.intro hNJ hL)
      have hL' : Legs KiKi := by
        cases hdisj with
        | inl hT =>
            exact A5 KiKi (And.intro A6 hT)
        | inr hLeg =>
            exact hLeg
      exact hL hL'
  -- From jumping and being an animal, Jane likes KiKi.
  have kiki_liked : LikesJane KiKi := A3 KiKi (And.intro A6 kiki_jumps)
  -- Therefore, KiKi does not bark (else Jane would both like and not like KiKi).
  have kiki_not_bark : ¬ Bark KiKi := by
    intro hBark
    have hnotlike : ¬ LikesJane KiKi := A1 KiKi (And.intro A6 hBark)
    exact hnotlike kiki_liked
  -- From "dogs bark," KiKi is not a dog.
  have kiki_not_dog : ¬ Dog KiKi := by
    intro hDog
    have hBark : Bark KiKi := (A2 KiKi hDog).right
    exact kiki_not_bark hBark
  exact And.intro kiki_not_bark kiki_not_dog
</lean>

ANSWER: True

<lean>
axiom Alg : Type
axiom Supervised : Alg → Prop
axiom Unsupervised : Alg → Prop
axiom Reinforcement : Alg → Prop
axiom ML : Alg → Prop
axiom RequiresLabels : Alg → Prop

-- ML algorithms are categorized into supervised, unsupervised, or reinforcement
axiom ml_categorized : ∀ a : Alg, ML a → (Supervised a ∨ Unsupervised a ∨ Reinforcement a)

-- Unsupervised algorithms do not require labeled data
axiom unsupervised_no_labels : ∀ a : Alg, Unsupervised a → ¬ RequiresLabels a

-- The algorithm used to train the state-of-the-art text summarization model
axiom sotaAlg : Alg
axiom sota_is_ml : ML sotaAlg
axiom sota_requires_labels : RequiresLabels sotaAlg
axiom sota_not_reinforcement : ¬ Reinforcement sotaAlg

-- Conclusion: the SoTA model is trained with supervised learning
theorem sota_supervised : Supervised sotaAlg := by
  have h := ml_categorized sotaAlg sota_is_ml
  cases h with
  | inl hs => exact hs
  | inr h2 =>
    cases h2 with
    | inl hu =>
      have hnl : ¬ RequiresLabels sotaAlg := unsupervised_no_labels sotaAlg hu
      have contra : False := hnl sota_requires_labels
      exact False.elim contra
    | inr hr =>
      exact False.elim (sota_not_reinforcement hr)
</lean>

ANSWER: True

axiom Ekaterina : Person

axiom CanRegisterUS : Person → Prop
axiom Participate2024 : Person → Prop
axiom USCitizen : Person → Prop
axiom TaiwaneseCitizen : Person → Prop
axiom RussianOfficial : Person → Prop
axiom ManagerGazprom : Person → Prop

-- 1. Everyone who can register to vote in the US can participate in the 2024 US presidential election.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : Person, CanRegisterUS x → Participate2024 x
-- 2. If someone has US citizenship, then they can register to vote in the US.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : Person, USCitizen x → CanRegisterUS x
-- 3. A person either has US citizenship or Taiwanese citizenship.
axiom R3 : ∀ x : Person, USCitizen x ∨ TaiwaneseCitizen x
-- 4. No Russian Federation officials hold Taiwanese citizenship.
axiom R4 : ∀ x : Person, RussianOfficial x → ¬TaiwaneseCitizen x
-- 5. Vladimir neither holds Taiwanese citizenship nor is he a manager at Gazprom.
axiom F1 : ¬TaiwaneseCitizen Vladimir ∧ ¬ManagerGazprom Vladimir
-- 6. Ekaterina can register to vote in the US, or she is a Russian federation official.
axiom E1 : CanRegisterUS Ekaterina ∨ RussianOfficial Ekaterina

-- Some consequences from the context
theorem vladimir_not_taiwanese : ¬TaiwaneseCitizen Vladimir := by
  exact And.left F1

theorem vladimir_us_citizen : USCitizen Vladimir := by
  have hnt : ¬TaiwaneseCitizen Vladimir := And.left F1
  have h := R3 Vladimir
  cases h with
  | inl hUS => exact hUS
  | inr hTW =>
      have : False := hnt hTW
      exact False.elim this

theorem vladimir_can_register : CanRegisterUS Vladimir := by
  exact R2 Vladimir vladimir_us_citizen

theorem vladimir_can_participate : Participate2024 Vladimir := by
  exact R1 Vladimir vladimir_can_register

-- From the given axioms, we cannot derive RussianOfficial Vladimir nor its negation.
-- There is no axiom linking non-Taiwanese to being an official; only officials ⇒ non-Taiwanese.
-- Hence, the status of Vladimir being a Russian federation official is underdetermined.
</lean>

ANSWER: Uncertain

      have nsVol : ¬Volatile KO := by intro hv; exact False.elim f

-- Axioms from the context
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ PerformsOften x → AttendsEngaged x
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x → (PerformsOften x ∨ InactiveDisinterested x)
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ Chaperones x → ¬ Student x
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ InactiveDisinterested x → Chaperones x
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ YoungChildOrTeen x ∧ WishesFurtherAcad x → Student x

-- Target statement (to be shown false):
-- If Bonnie either chaperones high school dances or, if not, she performs often,
-- then she is both (a young child or teen who wishes to further academics) and inactive/disinterested.
def Antecedent : Prop :=
  (Chaperones Bonnie) ∨ ((¬ Chaperones Bonnie) → PerformsOften Bonnie)

def Consequent : Prop :=
  (YoungChildOrTeen Bonnie ∧ WishesFurtherAcad Bonnie) ∧ InactiveDisinterested Bonnie

theorem statement_false : ¬ (Antecedent → Consequent) := by
  intro hImp
  -- From club membership, Bonnie either performs often or is inactive/disinterested
  have hClub : InClub Bonnie := A6
  have hPO_or_I : PerformsOften Bonnie ∨ InactiveDisinterested Bonnie := A2 Bonnie hClub
  -- Build the antecedent from the disjunction above
  have hAnt : Antecedent := by
    cases hPO_or_I with
    | inl hPO =>
      apply Or.inr
      intro _
      exact hPO
    | inr hI =>
      have hCh : Chaperones Bonnie := A4 Bonnie ⟨hClub, hI⟩
      exact Or.inl hCh
  -- Apply the implication to get the consequent
  have hCons : Consequent := hImp hAnt
  -- From the consequent, derive Student and ¬Student, contradiction
  have hYW : YoungChildOrTeen Bonnie ∧ WishesFurtherAcad Bonnie := And.left hCons
  have hI : InactiveDisinterested Bonnie := And.right hCons
  have hStud : Student Bonnie := A5 Bonnie ⟨hClub, hYW.left, hYW.right⟩
  have hCh : Chaperones Bonnie := A4 Bonnie ⟨hClub, hI⟩
  have hNotStud : ¬ Student Bonnie := A3 Bonnie ⟨hClub, hCh⟩
  exact hNotStud hStud
</lean>

axiom BakedSweet : obj → Prop
axiom Spicy : obj → Prop
axiom Cupcake : obj → Prop
axiom MalaHotpot : obj → Prop
axiom ProductOfBBM : obj → Prop

-- No baked sweets are spicy.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, BakedSweet x → ¬ Spicy x
-- All cupcakes are baked sweets.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Cupcake x → BakedSweet x
-- All mala hotpots are spicy.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, MalaHotpot x → Spicy x
-- All products from Baked by Melissa are cupcakes.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, ProductOfBBM x → Cupcake x
-- Dried Thai chilies are spicy or mala hotpots or not baked sweets.
axiom A5 : Spicy DriedThaiChilies ∨ (MalaHotpot DriedThaiChilies ∨ ¬ BakedSweet DriedThaiChilies)

theorem not_bbm_dtc : ¬ ProductOfBBM DriedThaiChilies := by
  intro hBBM
  have hCup : Cupcake DriedThaiChilies := A4 DriedThaiChilies hBBM
  have hBaked : BakedSweet DriedThaiChilies := A2 DriedThaiChilies hCup
  have hNotSpicy : ¬ Spicy DriedThaiChilies := A1 DriedThaiChilies hBaked
  have hNotMala : ¬ MalaHotpot DriedThaiChilies := by
    intro hM
    have hS : Spicy DriedThaiChilies := A3 DriedThaiChilies hM
    exact hNotSpicy hS
  have hNotNotBaked : ¬ (¬ BakedSweet DriedThaiChilies) := by
    intro hnb
    exact hnb hBaked
  have : False := by
    cases A5 with
    | inl hS => exact hNotSpicy hS
    | inr h =>
      cases h with
      | inl hM => exact hNotMala hM
      | inr hnb => exact hNotNotBaked hnb
  exact this
</lean>

axiom obj : Type

-- Predicates
axiom InClub : obj → Prop
axiom PerformsOften : obj → Prop
axiom AttendsEngagedEvents : obj → Prop
axiom InactiveDisinterested : obj → Prop
axiom ChaperonesDances : obj → Prop
axiom StudentAttendsSchool : obj → Prop
axiom YoungChild : obj → Prop
axiom Teenager : obj → Prop
axiom WishesFurtherEd : obj → Prop

-- Axioms from the context
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ PerformsOften x → AttendsEngagedEvents x
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x → (PerformsOften x ∨ InactiveDisinterested x)
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ ChaperonesDances x → ¬ StudentAttendsSchool x
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ InactiveDisinterested x → ChaperonesDances x
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ (YoungChild x ∨ Teenager x) ∧ WishesFurtherEd x → StudentAttendsSchool x

-- Bonnie and her special disjunction
axiom Bonnie : obj
axiom Bonnie_inClub : InClub Bonnie
axiom Bonnie_link :
  (AttendsEngagedEvents Bonnie ∧ StudentAttendsSchool Bonnie) ∨
  (¬ AttendsEngagedEvents Bonnie ∧ ¬ StudentAttendsSchool Bonnie)

-- Useful derived lemmas
lemma inactive_implies_not_student (x : obj) (hc : InClub x) (hi : InactiveDisinterested x) :
  ¬ StudentAttendsSchool x := by
  have hc2 : InClub x ∧ InactiveDisinterested x := And.intro hc hi
  have hch : ChaperonesDances x := A4 x hc2
  have hc3 : InClub x ∧ ChaperonesDances x := And.intro hc hch
  exact A3 x hc3

lemma student_implies_not_inactive (x : obj) (hc : InClub x) (hs : StudentAttendsSchool x) :
  ¬ InactiveDisinterested x := by
  intro hi
  have hns := inactive_implies_not_student x hc hi
  exact hns hs

-- If Bonnie is a student, then she performs often
lemma bonnie_student_implies_performs : StudentAttendsSchool Bonnie → PerformsOften Bonnie := by
  intro hs
  have hc : InClub Bonnie := Bonnie_inClub
  have pori : PerformsOften Bonnie ∨ InactiveDisinterested Bonnie := A2 Bonnie hc
  have hni : ¬ InactiveDisinterested Bonnie := student_implies_not_inactive Bonnie hc hs
  exact Or.elim pori (fun hp => hp) (fun hi => False.elim (hni hi))

-- If Bonnie is not a student, then she does not attend-engaged
lemma bonnie_not_student_implies_not_attends :
  ¬ StudentAttendsSchool Bonnie → ¬ AttendsEngagedEvents Bonnie := by
  intro hns
  intro ha
  have d := Bonnie_link
  apply Or.elim d
  · intro h1
    have hs : StudentAttendsSchool Bonnie := And.right h1
    exact hns hs
  · intro h2
    have hna : ¬ AttendsEngagedEvents Bonnie := And.left h2
    exact hna ha

-- Performances imply attending/engaged for Bonnie (from club rule)
lemma bonnie_perform_implies_attend :
  PerformsOften Bonnie → AttendsEngagedEvents Bonnie := by
  intro hp
  have hc : InClub Bonnie := Bonnie_inClub
  have hpair : InClub Bonnie ∧ PerformsOften Bonnie := And.intro hc hp
  exact A1 Bonnie hpair

-- If Bonnie is not a student, then she does not perform often
lemma bonnie_not_student_implies_not_performs :
  ¬ StudentAttendsSchool Bonnie → ¬ PerformsOften Bonnie := by
  intro hns
  intro hp
  have ha : AttendsEngagedEvents Bonnie := bonnie_perform_implies_attend hp
  have hna := bonnie_not_student_implies_not_attends hns
  exact hna ha

-- From the given information, we cannot derive PerformsOften Bonnie nor its negation.
-- No theorem proving or refuting PerformsOften Bonnie is derivable without additional assumptions.
</lean>

axiom InClub : obj → Prop
axiom PerformsOften : obj → Prop
axiom AttendsEngagedEvents : obj → Prop
axiom InactiveDisinterested : obj → Prop
axiom ChaperonesDances : obj → Prop
axiom StudentAttendsSchool : obj → Prop
axiom YoungChild : obj → Prop
axiom Teenager : obj → Prop
axiom WishesFurtherEd : obj → Prop

-- Axioms from the context
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ PerformsOften x → AttendsEngagedEvents x
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x → (PerformsOften x ∨ InactiveDisinterested x)
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ ChaperonesDances x → ¬ StudentAttendsSchool x
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ InactiveDisinterested x → ChaperonesDances x
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, InClub x ∧ (YoungChild x ∨ Teenager x) ∧ WishesFurtherEd x → StudentAttendsSchool x

-- Bonnie and her special disjunction
axiom Bonnie : obj
axiom Bonnie_inClub : InClub Bonnie
axiom Bonnie_link :
  (AttendsEngagedEvents Bonnie ∧ StudentAttendsSchool Bonnie) ∨
  (¬ AttendsEngagedEvents Bonnie ∧ ¬ StudentAttendsSchool Bonnie)

-- Useful derived lemmas
lemma inactive_implies_not_student (x : obj) (hc : InClub x) (hi : InactiveDisinterested x) :
  ¬ StudentAttendsSchool x := by
  have hc2 : InClub x ∧ InactiveDisinterested x := And.intro hc hi
  have hch : ChaperonesDances x := A4 x hc2
  have hc3 : InClub x ∧ ChaperonesDances x := And.intro hc hch
  exact A3 x hc3

lemma student_implies_not_inactive (x : obj) (hc : InClub x) (hs : StudentAttendsSchool x) :
  ¬ InactiveDisinterested x := by
  intro hi
  have hns := inactive_implies_not_student x hc hi
  exact hns hs

-- Performances imply attending/engaged for Bonnie (from club rule)
lemma bonnie_perform_implies_attend :
  PerformsOften Bonnie → AttendsEngagedEvents Bonnie := by
  intro hp
  have hc : InClub Bonnie := Bonnie_inClub
  have hpair : InClub Bonnie ∧ PerformsOften Bonnie := And.intro hc hp
  exact A1 Bonnie hpair

-- If Bonnie is not a student, then she does not attend/engage
lemma bonnie_not_student_implies_not_attends :
  ¬ StudentAttendsSchool Bonnie → ¬ AttendsEngagedEvents Bonnie := by
  intro hns ha
  have d := Bonnie_link
  -- Build ¬AttendsEngagedEvents Bonnie from the disjunction
  have hna : ¬ AttendsEngagedEvents Bonnie :=
    Or.elim d
      (fun h1 => False.elim (hns h1.right))
      (fun h2 => h2.left)
  exact hna ha

-- If Bonnie is a student, then she performs often
lemma bonnie_student_implies_performs : StudentAttendsSchool Bonnie → PerformsOften Bonnie := by
  intro hs
  have hc : InClub Bonnie := Bonnie_inClub
  have pori : PerformsOften Bonnie ∨ InactiveDisinterested Bonnie := A2 Bonnie hc
  have hni : ¬ InactiveDisinterested Bonnie := student_implies_not_inactive Bonnie hc hs
  exact Or.elim pori (fun hp => hp) (fun hi => False.elim (hni hi))

-- If Bonnie is not a student, then she does not perform often
lemma bonnie_not_student_implies_not_performs :
  ¬ StudentAttendsSchool Bonnie → ¬ PerformsOften Bonnie := by
  intro hns hp
  have ha : AttendsEngagedEvents Bonnie := bonnie_perform_implies_attend hp
  have hna := bonnie_not_student_implies_not_attends hns
  exact hna ha

-- From the given information, we cannot derive PerformsOften Bonnie nor its negation.
-- Hence, the truth of PerformsOften Bonnie is uncertain.
</lean>

ANSWER: Uncertain

<lean>
axiom obj : Type
axiom DriedThaiChilies : obj

axiom Spicy : obj → Prop
axiom BakedSweet : obj → Prop
axiom Cupcake : obj → Prop
axiom MalaHotpot : obj → Prop
axiom FromBBM : obj → Prop

-- No baked sweets are spicy.
axiom NoBakedSweetSpicy : ∀ x : obj, BakedSweet x → ¬ Spicy x
-- All cupcakes are baked sweets.
axiom CupcakeIsBakedSweet : ∀ x : obj, Cupcake x → BakedSweet x
-- All mala hotpots are spicy.
axiom MalaIsSpicy : ∀ x : obj, MalaHotpot x → Spicy x
-- All products from Baked by Melissa are cupcakes.
axiom BBMProductsAreCupcakes : ∀ x : obj, FromBBM x → Cupcake x
-- Dried Thai chilies are spicy or mala hotpots or not baked sweets.
axiom DTC_disj : Spicy DriedThaiChilies ∨ MalaHotpot DriedThaiChilies ∨ ¬ BakedSweet DriedThaiChilies

theorem dried_thai_chilies_not_BBM : ¬ FromBBM DriedThaiChilies :=
by
  have hCup : FromBBM DriedThaiChilies → Cupcake DriedThaiChilies :=
    BBMProductsAreCupcakes DriedThaiChilies
  have hBake : FromBBM DriedThaiChilies → BakedSweet DriedThaiChilies :=
    fun h => CupcakeIsBakedSweet DriedThaiChilies (hCup h)
  have hNotSpicy : FromBBM DriedThaiChilies → ¬ Spicy DriedThaiChilies :=
    fun h => NoBakedSweetSpicy DriedThaiChilies (hBake h)
  cases DTC_disj with
  | inl hs =>
      intro hBBM
      exact (hNotSpicy hBBM) hs
  | inr h2 =>
      cases h2 with
      | inl hm =>
          have hs : Spicy DriedThaiChilies := MalaIsSpicy DriedThaiChilies hm
          intro hBBM
          exact (hNotSpicy hBBM) hs
      | inr hnotBake =>
          intro hBBM
          exact hnotBake (hBake hBBM)
</lean>

ANSWER: True

axiom obj : Type

axiom Animal : obj → Prop
axiom Mammal : obj → Prop
axiom Human : obj → Prop

axiom HasMonkeypox : obj → Prop

axiom Fever : obj → Prop
axiom Headache : obj → Prop
axiom MusclePains : obj → Prop
axiom Tired : obj → Prop
axiom Flu : obj → Prop

-- When the Monkeypox virus occurs in a being, it may get Monkeypox.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, OccursMPXV x → HasMonkeypox x

-- Monkeypox virus can occur in certain animals.
axiom A1 : ∃ x : obj, Animal x ∧ OccursMPXV x

-- Humans are mammals.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Human x → Mammal x

-- Mammals are animals.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Mammal x → Animal x

-- Symptoms of Monkeypox include fever, headache, muscle pains, and tiredness.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, HasMonkeypox x → Fever x ∧ Headache x ∧ MusclePains x ∧ Tired x

-- People feel tired when they get the flu.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Flu x → Tired x

theorem there_is_an_animal : ∃ x : obj, Animal x := by
  obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := A1
  exact ⟨x, And.left hx⟩
</lean>

theorem mixture_not_chemical_and_not_oneelement : ¬ Chemical Mixture ∧ ¬ OneElement Mixture := by
  have hcontra : ¬ (Chemical Mixture ∧ OneElement Mixture) := by
    intro h
    have hc : Chemical Mixture := h.left
    have ho : OneElement Mixture := h.right
    have hno : ¬ OneElement Mixture := A5 Mixture hc
    exact hno ho
  cases A6 with
  | inl h =>
    have f : False := hcontra h
    have hnc : ¬ Chemical Mixture := by
      intro hc
      exact False.elim f
    have hno : ¬ OneElement Mixture := by
      intro ho
      exact False.elim f
    exact And.intro hnc hno
  | inr h =>
    exact h

theorem mixture_not_chemical : ¬ Chemical Mixture :=
  (mixture_not_chemical_and_not_oneelement).left

theorem mixture_not_oneelement : ¬ OneElement Mixture :=
  (mixture_not_chemical_and_not_oneelement).right

theorem mixture_not_organic : ¬ Organic Mixture := by
  intro hOrg
  have hChem : Chemical Mixture := A3 Mixture hOrg
  exact mixture_not_chemical hChem

<lean>
axiom obj : Type
axiom Mixture : obj

axiom Hydrocarbon : obj → Prop
axiom Organic : obj → Prop
axiom Alkane : obj → Prop
axiom ChemicalCompound : obj → Prop
axiom ContainsCarbon : obj → Prop
axiom ContainsOnlyOneElement : obj → Prop

-- All hydrocarbons are organic compounds.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Hydrocarbon x → Organic x
-- All alkanes are hydrocarbons.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Alkane x → Hydrocarbon x
-- All organic compounds are chemical compounds.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Organic x → ChemicalCompound x
-- All organic compounds contain carbon.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Organic x → ContainsCarbon x
-- No chemical compounds contain only one element.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, ChemicalCompound x → ¬ ContainsOnlyOneElement x
-- The mixture either both is a chemical compound and contains only one element, or neither.
axiom A6 : (ChemicalCompound Mixture ∧ ContainsOnlyOneElement Mixture) ∨
           (¬ChemicalCompound Mixture ∧ ¬ContainsOnlyOneElement Mixture)

-- From the chain Alkane → Hydrocarbon → Organic → ChemicalCompound
lemma alkane_implies_chemical : ∀ x : obj, Alkane x → ChemicalCompound x := by
  intro x hAlk
  have hHyd : Hydrocarbon x := A2 x hAlk
  have hOrg : Organic x := A1 x hHyd
  exact A3 x hOrg

-- The mixture is not a chemical compound.
lemma mixture_not_chemical : ¬ChemicalCompound Mixture := by
  intro hChem
  have hNotOne : ¬ContainsOnlyOneElement Mixture := A5 Mixture hChem
  have hdisj := A6
  cases hdisj with
  | inl hboth =>
      -- hboth: ChemicalCompound Mixture ∧ ContainsOnlyOneElement Mixture
      exact hNotOne hboth.right
  | inr hneither =>
      -- hneither: ¬ChemicalCompound Mixture ∧ ¬ContainsOnlyOneElement Mixture
      exact hneither.left hChem

-- Hence, the mixture is not an alkane (since alkanes are chemical compounds).
lemma mixture_not_alkane : ¬Alkane Mixture := by
  intro hAlk
  have hChem : ChemicalCompound Mixture := alkane_implies_chemical Mixture hAlk
  exact mixture_not_chemical hChem

-- Main statement: If the mixture contains only one element or contains carbon,
-- then the mixture is neither a chemical compound nor an alkane.
theorem mixture_claim :
  (ContainsOnlyOneElement Mixture ∨ ContainsCarbon Mixture) →
  (¬ChemicalCompound Mixture ∧ ¬Alkane Mixture) := by
  intro _
  exact And.intro mixture_not_chemical mixture_not_alkane
</lean>

ANSWER: True

-- All students taking the database course are from CS.

-- All students taking a class with Professor David are taking the database course.
axiom R4 : ∀ x : obj, WithDavid x → Database x
-- All students working in the lab are taking a class with Professor David.

-- James is a student working in the lab.
axiom T1 : WorkLab James
-- James doesn't work in the lab or have a part-time job offered by the university.
axiom T2 : ¬ (WorkLab James ∨ PartTime James)

theorem james_not_parttime : ¬ PartTime James :=
  fun hpt =>
    have hlib : WorkLibrary James := R2 James hpt
    have hnotcs : ¬ CS James := R1 James hlib
    have hdavid : WithDavid James := R5 James T1
    have hdb : Database James := R4 James hdavid
    have hcs : CS James := R3 James hdb
    show False from hnotcs hcs

  intro himp
